name: HDL Cholesterol (HDL-C)
summary: HDL cholesterol is the cholesterol carried by high-density lipoprotein particles, often called "good" cholesterol because HDL helps transport cholesterol away from artery walls back to the liver. It is reported directly on a standard lipid panel.
whatItMeans: For HDL, higher is generally considered protective and lower is associated with greater cardiovascular risk — the opposite direction from LDL and triglycerides. Low HDL frequently accompanies insulin resistance, obesity, and high triglycerides. Very high HDL does not necessarily add further protection, and extremely high levels have been linked in some studies to atypical risk, so context matters.
raises: Moderate alcohol intake (not recommended solely to raise HDL)
Healthy unsaturated fats (e.g. olive oil, fatty fish)
confounders: High triglycerides can interfere with some HDL assay methods
Recent acute illness or inflammation can transiently alter HDL
Pregnancy and hormonal changes shift HDL
HDL is relatively stable in the non-fasting state compared with triglycerides

ranges.note: General: < 40 mg/dL is considered low; ≥ 60 mg/dL is considered protective (NCEP ATP III).
Men: < 40 mg/dL is low; ≥ 60 mg/dL is optimal/protective.
Women: < 50 mg/dL is low; ≥ 60 mg/dL is optimal/protective.

references.title: Cholesterol testing and results: MedlinePlus Medical Encyclopedia
